title: hieracium scorzonerifolium subsp. hodzae
intro: Hieracium scorzonerifolium subsp. hodzae, also known as a subspecies of Hawkweed, is a flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Hieracium scorzonerifolium subsp. hodzae can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
